question: How many times a day are Muslims required to pray?
explanation: Muslims are obligated to perform five daily prayers (Salah): Fajr, Dhuhr, Asr, Maghrib, and Isha. This is one of the Five Pillars of Islam and is obligatory upon every sane, adult Muslim.

source.name: Quran, Surah Al-Baqarah
source.reference: Surah Al-Baqarah 2:238

question: What is the minimum number of body parts that must be washed during wudu (ablution)?
explanation: The Quran specifies four obligatory acts of wudu: washing the face, washing both arms up to the elbows, wiping over the head, and washing both feet up to the ankles. These four are the fard (obligatory) elements of wudu.

source.name: Quran, Surah Al-Maidah
source.reference: Surah Al-Maidah 5:6

question: Which pillar of Islam involves fasting during the month of Ramadan?

options.A: Zakat
options.B: Hajj
options.C: Salah
options.D: Sawm

explanation: Sawm refers to the obligatory fast during the month of Ramadan. Muslims abstain from food, drink, and other invalidating acts from dawn (Fajr) until sunset (Maghrib). It is the fourth pillar of Islam.

source.name: Quran, Surah Al-Baqarah
source.reference: Surah Al-Baqarah 2:183-185

question: What must a Muslim face during prayer?

options.A: Madinah
options.B: Jerusalem
options.C: The Qibla (direction of the Kaaba in Makkah)
options.D: The rising sun

explanation: Muslims must face the Qibla — the direction of the Kaaba in Makkah — during their prayers. This was established as a command in the Quran and is a condition for the validity of the prayer.

source.name: Quran, Surah Al-Baqarah
source.reference: Surah Al-Baqarah 2:144

question: What is the term for the obligatory annual charity paid on wealth that meets the nisab threshold?

options.A: Sadaqah
options.B: Zakat
options.C: Waqf
options.D: Kaffarah

explanation: Zakat is the obligatory annual charity given on qualifying wealth held for a full lunar year above the nisab threshold. It is one of the Five Pillars of Islam and is distributed to eight categories of recipients mentioned in the Quran.

source.name: Quran, Surah At-Tawbah
source.reference: Surah At-Tawbah 9:60

question: Which prayer is performed on Friday as a congregational obligation for Muslim men?

options.A: Tahajjud
options.B: Salat al-Eid
options.C: Salat al-Jumuah
options.D: Salat al-Duha

explanation: Salat al-Jumuah (Friday prayer) is an obligatory congregational prayer for adult Muslim men, replacing the Dhuhr prayer on Fridays. It includes a khutbah (sermon) before two units of prayer. The Quran explicitly commands believers to hasten to the remembrance of Allah on Friday.

source.name: Quran, Surah Al-Jumuah
source.reference: Surah Al-Jumuah 62:9

question: Which of the following actions invalidates wudu (ablution)?

options.A: Eating cooked food
options.B: Deep sleep in a lying position
options.C: Speaking loudly
options.D: Crying

explanation: Deep sleep, especially in a lying position, invalidates wudu because one loses consciousness and control over bodily functions. Eating cooked food does not invalidate wudu per the majority opinion. Speaking and crying do not invalidate wudu.

source.name: Sahih Muslim
source.reference: Book 2, Hadith 469

question: What is the ruling on a Muslim who intentionally misses a Fard (obligatory) prayer without a valid excuse?

options.A: It is makruh (disliked) but can be made up easily
options.B: It is a major sin and must be made up (qada) as soon as possible
options.C: The prayer is simply lost and cannot be made up
options.D: A monetary penalty (kaffarah) must be paid

explanation: Intentionally missing a fard prayer without a valid reason is considered a major sin in Islam. The majority of scholars hold that the prayer must still be made up (qada) as soon as possible, alongside sincere repentance. Neglecting prayer entirely is considered one of the gravest sins.

source.name: Sahih al-Bukhari; scholarly consensus
source.reference: Sahih al-Bukhari Book 9, Hadith 627; Ibn Qudamah, Al-Mughni

question: How many rak'ahs (units of prayer) does the Fajr prayer consist of?

options.A: 4 fard
options.B: 3 fard
options.C: 2 fard
options.D: 2 sunnah and 4 fard

explanation: The Fajr prayer consists of 2 fard (obligatory) rak'ahs. It is also preceded by 2 sunnah rak'ahs, which the Prophet (SAW) said were more beloved to him than the world and everything in it, but these sunnah rak'ahs are not fard.

source.name: Sahih al-Bukhari
source.reference: Book 11, Hadith 625

question: What is the rate of Zakat that must be paid on gold, silver, and cash savings?
explanation: The obligatory rate of Zakat on gold, silver, and cash savings is 2.5% (one-fortieth) of the total wealth that has been held above the nisab threshold for one full lunar year (hawl). This rate is established by authentic hadith and scholarly consensus.

source.name: Sahih al-Bukhari
source.reference: Book 24, Hadith 534

question: Which of the following people is NOT among the eight categories eligible to receive Zakat?

options.A: A person in debt
options.B: A wealthy businessman travelling abroad
options.C: A new Muslim whose heart is to be reconciled
options.D: A stranded traveller with no funds

explanation: The eight categories eligible for Zakat are listed in Surah At-Tawbah 9:60: the poor, the needy, Zakat administrators, new Muslims (muallafat al-qulub), those in bondage, the indebted, in the cause of Allah, and the stranded traveller (ibn as-sabil). A wealthy businessman, even abroad, does not qualify as he has access to his wealth.

source.name: Quran, Surah At-Tawbah
source.reference: Surah At-Tawbah 9:60

question: What is Tayammum and when is it permitted?

options.A: A shortened prayer performed during travel
options.B: Dry ablution using clean earth or dust, permitted when water is unavailable or its use would cause harm
options.C: A supererogatory prayer performed after Isha
options.D: Wiping over leather socks instead of washing the feet in wudu

explanation: Tayammum is the Islamic practice of dry ablution using clean earth or dust as a substitute for wudu or ghusl. It is permitted when water is unavailable, inaccessible, or when using water would cause harm to health. The procedure involves striking clean earth with both hands and wiping the face and hands.

source.name: Quran, Surah Al-Maidah; Sahih al-Bukhari
source.reference: Surah Al-Maidah 5:6; Sahih al-Bukhari Book 7, Hadith 334

question: According to the majority of scholars, what is the minimum amount of gold (nisab) that makes Zakat obligatory?

options.A: 100 grams
options.B: 70 grams
options.C: 85 grams
options.D: 92.5 grams

explanation: The nisab for gold is 20 mithqal, equivalent to approximately 85 grams according to the Hanafi school and the majority of contemporary scholars. If one possesses this amount or its equivalent in wealth for a full lunar year, Zakat becomes obligatory at 2.5%.

source.name: Reliance of the Traveller; Sahih al-Bukhari
source.reference: Reliance of the Traveller h1.4; Sahih al-Bukhari Book 24, Hadith 534

question: In Hanafi fiqh, what is the ruling on performing Salah behind an imam who belongs to a different madhab?

options.A: It is completely invalid and must be repeated
options.B: It is valid as long as the imam's prayer is valid according to his own madhab
options.C: It is only valid if the follower does not know the imam's madhab
options.D: It is makruh tahrimi (prohibitively disliked) in all cases

explanation: According to the Hanafi madhab and the position of the majority of scholars, it is valid to pray behind an imam of a different madhab, provided that the imam's prayer is valid according to his own school of jurisprudence. This position upholds unity in the Muslim community and is based on the principle that the follower's prayer is dependent on the imam's.

source.name: Fatawa Alamgiri; Ibn Abidin, Radd al-Muhtar
source.reference: Fatawa Alamgiri Vol. 1; Radd al-Muhtar ala al-Durr al-Mukhtar, Book of Prayer

question: What is the difference between Fard Ayn and Fard Kifayah in Islamic law?

options.A: Fard Ayn is obligatory only for scholars; Fard Kifayah is for all Muslims
options.B: Fard Ayn is an individual obligation on every Muslim; Fard Kifayah is a collective obligation discharged when enough members of the community perform it
options.C: Fard Ayn refers to obligatory acts of worship; Fard Kifayah refers to recommended acts
options.D: Fard Ayn is obligatory in Ramadan only; Fard Kifayah applies year-round

explanation: Fard Ayn is an individual obligation that every legally responsible Muslim must personally fulfil, such as the five daily prayers and fasting Ramadan. Fard Kifayah is a collective duty that, when fulfilled by a sufficient number in the community, removes the obligation from the rest — such as performing the funeral prayer (Salat al-Janazah) or returning a greeting of salam.

source.name: Reliance of the Traveller; Usul al-Fiqh works of Imam al-Ghazali
source.reference: Reliance of the Traveller a1.1-a1.2; Al-Mustasfa by Imam al-Ghazali

question: Under which conditions is it permissible to perform Salah while seated, according to classical fiqh?

options.A: Only when aboard a moving vehicle
options.B: Whenever one feels tired or the prayer is long
options.C: When one is genuinely unable to stand due to illness or severe harm that standing would cause
options.D: Only for elderly people above the age of 60

explanation: Standing (qiyam) is a pillar of the fard prayer. However, if a person is genuinely unable to stand due to illness, injury, or fear that standing would significantly worsen their condition, they may pray seated. If they cannot sit, they may pray lying on their side. The threshold is genuine inability or fear of serious harm, not mere discomfort or tiredness.

source.name: Sahih al-Bukhari; Reliance of the Traveller
source.reference: Sahih al-Bukhari Book 19, Hadith 1117; Reliance of the Traveller f8.1

question: What is the ruling on Zakat al-Fitr and when must it be paid?

options.A: It is sunnah and can be paid any time during Ramadan
options.B: It is wajib and must be paid before the Eid al-Fitr prayer, with the amount being one sa' of staple food per person
options.C: It is fard and equals 2.5% of one's annual savings
options.D: It is only obligatory on the head of the household and not for dependants

explanation: Zakat al-Fitr is wajib (obligatory according to Hanafi scholars) or fard (according to the majority) upon every Muslim who possesses more than their basic needs on the day of Eid. It must be paid before the Eid al-Fitr prayer. The amount is one sa' (approximately 2.5–3 kg) of the local staple food per person in one's household, including dependants. Its purpose is to purify the fasting person and provide for the poor on Eid.

source.name: Sahih al-Bukhari; Sahih Muslim
source.reference: Sahih al-Bukhari Book 25, Hadith 579; Sahih Muslim Book 12, Hadith 984

question: In Islamic inheritance law (Ilm al-Faraid), what share of the estate does a daughter receive when she is the sole heir with no male siblings?

options.A: One quarter (1/4)
options.B: One third (1/3)
options.C: One half (1/2)
options.D: Two thirds (2/3)

explanation: According to Surah An-Nisa, if a man leaves behind a single daughter with no son, she inherits one half (1/2) of the estate as a fixed share (fard). If there are two or more daughters and no sons, they together receive two-thirds (2/3). If there is a son alongside daughters, the rule of asabah applies and the son receives double the share of a daughter.

source.name: Quran, Surah An-Nisa
source.reference: Surah An-Nisa 4:11

question: What is Ghusl in Islamic law?

options.A: The dry ablution using sand or earth
options.B: The full ritual bath of the entire body required to remove major ritual impurity
options.C: The washing of hands and face before eating
options.D: The partial washing performed before prayer

explanation: Ghusl is the full ritual bath in which water must reach every part of the body including the hair roots. It is required to remove major ritual impurity (janabah) caused by marital relations, ejaculation, menstruation, or postnatal bleeding. It is also recommended before Jumu'ah, Eid prayers, and entering the state of ihram for Hajj or Umrah.

source.name: Quran, Surah Al-Maidah; Sahih al-Bukhari
source.reference: Surah Al-Maidah 5:6; Sahih al-Bukhari Book 5, Hadith 248

question: Which of the following foods is clearly forbidden (haram) in Islam?

options.A: Beef
options.B: Chicken
options.C: Pork
options.D: Fish

explanation: Pork (the flesh of swine) is explicitly forbidden in the Quran in multiple verses. Allah states that He has forbidden carrion, blood, pork, and animals slaughtered in a name other than Allah's. Fish and seafood are generally permissible, while beef and chicken are halal when properly slaughtered with the name of Allah.

source.name: Quran, Surah Al-Baqarah; Surah Al-Maidah
source.reference: Surah Al-Baqarah 2:173; Surah Al-Maidah 5:3

question: What is the correct order of the five daily prayers?

options.A: Fajr, Dhuhr, Asr, Isha, Maghrib
options.B: Fajr, Asr, Dhuhr, Maghrib, Isha
options.C: Fajr, Dhuhr, Asr, Maghrib, Isha
options.D: Dhuhr, Fajr, Asr, Isha, Maghrib

explanation: The five daily prayers in chronological order are: Fajr (pre-dawn), Dhuhr (midday), Asr (afternoon), Maghrib (after sunset), and Isha (night). Each prayer has a specific time window defined by the position of the sun. Praying within these windows is an obligation, and deliberately praying outside them without a valid excuse is a sin.

source.name: Quran, Surah An-Nisa; Sahih al-Bukhari
source.reference: Surah An-Nisa 4:103; Sahih al-Bukhari Book 9, Hadith 504

question: What is the term for the Islamic method of slaughtering animals for food?

options.A: Tayammum
options.B: Dhabihah (Zabihah)
options.C: Sadaqah
options.D: Qurbani

explanation: Dhabihah (or Zabihah) is the prescribed Islamic method of slaughter. It requires cutting the throat, windpipe, and blood vessels of the neck in a swift, humane manner while saying 'Bismillah, Allahu Akbar.' The blood must be fully drained. Animals slaughtered without mentioning Allah's name are not permissible for Muslims to eat.

source.name: Quran, Surah Al-Maidah; Sahih al-Bukhari
source.reference: Surah Al-Maidah 5:3-4; Sahih al-Bukhari Book 72, Hadith 5498

question: What is the ruling on shortening prayers (Qasr) during travel, and at what distance does it apply?

options.A: It is sunnah and applies once you have left your city limits regardless of distance
options.B: It is a concession (rukhsah) in which four-rak'ah prayers are reduced to two, applying at a travel distance of approximately 80km or more according to the majority of scholars
options.C: It is obligatory and applies to all prayers including Fajr and Maghrib
options.D: It is only permitted during Hajj and military expeditions

explanation: Qasr (shortening of prayers) is a divine concession granted to travellers. The four-rak'ah Fard prayers (Dhuhr, Asr, Isha) are reduced to two rak'ahs. The majority of scholars (Hanafi, Maliki, Shafi'i, Hanbali) set the minimum travel distance at approximately 48 miles (around 80km). The Quran explicitly mentions the permissibility of shortening prayers during travel.

source.name: Quran, Surah An-Nisa; Sahih al-Bukhari
source.reference: Surah An-Nisa 4:101; Sahih al-Bukhari Book 18, Hadith 1040

question: What is the concept of Mahram in Islamic law, and why is it significant?

options.A: It refers to the period of mourning after a death in the family
options.B: A Mahram is a male relative whom a woman is permanently forbidden to marry, with whom she may travel without restriction
options.C: It is the permission given by a judge for a couple to marry
options.D: A Mahram is any male Muslim who acts as a witness during a marriage contract

explanation: A Mahram is a man who is permanently forbidden to a woman in marriage due to close blood relation (such as father, brother, son), breastfeeding, or marriage (such as father-in-law). According to the majority of scholars, a woman should be accompanied by a Mahram when travelling long distances. The Prophet (SAW) said a woman should not travel a journey of three days except with a Mahram.

source.name: Sahih al-Bukhari; Sahih Muslim
source.reference: Sahih al-Bukhari Book 29, Hadith 1862; Sahih Muslim Book 15, Hadith 1341

question: What is Riba and why is it strictly prohibited in Islam?

options.A: Riba is any form of trade or commerce between Muslims and non-Muslims
options.B: Riba is interest or usury — any unjust increase in exchange of wealth — explicitly forbidden in the Quran in the strongest terms
options.C: Riba is the Islamic charity given to the poor which is recommended but not obligatory
options.D: Riba is the practice of hoarding gold and silver without paying Zakat

explanation: Riba refers to any unjust, exploitative increase in a loan or exchange transaction, broadly understood as interest and usury. It is one of the most severely prohibited things in Islam. The Quran states that those who deal in riba are waging war against Allah and His Messenger. The Prophet (SAW) cursed the one who takes riba, the one who gives it, the one who writes it, and the witnesses to it.

source.name: Quran, Surah Al-Baqarah; Sahih Muslim
source.reference: Surah Al-Baqarah 2:275-279; Sahih Muslim Book 22, Hadith 1598

question: What is the concept of Istihsan (juristic preference) in Islamic law, and which madhab is most associated with its use?

options.A: Istihsan is the principle of following the opinion of the majority of scholars; it is equally used by all four madhabs
options.B: Istihsan is a method of juristic preference where a jurist departs from strict analogical reasoning to a more equitable ruling; it is most associated with the Hanafi madhab
options.C: Istihsan refers to the scholarly consensus on a matter; it is primarily a Maliki concept
options.D: Istihsan means selecting the strongest hadith on a matter; it is a Shafi'i principle

explanation: Istihsan is a principle of Islamic jurisprudence in which a jurist departs from a strict analogical ruling (Qiyas) in favour of a more equitable, flexible alternative that better serves the objectives of Sharia. It is most prominently used in the Hanafi madhab, whose founder Imam Abu Hanifah extensively employed it. Imam al-Shafi'i famously opposed it, while Maliki scholars use a similar concept called Maslahah Mursalah.

source.name: Usul al-Fiqh works; Al-Muwafaqat by al-Shatibi
source.reference: Al-Muwafaqat fi Usul al-Shariah by Imam al-Shatibi; Imam Abu Hanifah's jurisprudence

question: What are the five essential objectives of Islamic law (Al-Maqasid al-Shariah) as identified by classical scholars?

options.A: Justice, equality, freedom, dignity, and prosperity
options.B: The protection of religion, life, intellect, lineage, and wealth
options.C: Worship, honesty, charity, community, and education
options.D: Tawhid, Sunnah, Fiqh, Akhlaq, and Dawah

explanation: The five Maqasid al-Shariah (higher objectives of Islamic law) as systematised by Imam al-Ghazali and others are: (1) Hifz al-Din — protection of religion, (2) Hifz al-Nafs — protection of life, (3) Hifz al-Aql — protection of intellect, (4) Hifz al-Nasl — protection of lineage/family, and (5) Hifz al-Mal — protection of wealth. All Islamic rulings are understood as serving these five objectives at various levels of necessity, need, or complementarity.

source.name: Al-Mustasfa by Imam al-Ghazali; Al-Muwafaqat by al-Shatibi
source.reference: Al-Mustasfa min Ilm al-Usul by Imam al-Ghazali; Al-Muwafaqat fi Usul al-Shariah by al-Shatibi

question: What is the ruling on combining prayers (Jam') during travel or due to hardship, and which prayers can be combined?

options.A: All five prayers can be combined into two sessions at any time if one is busy
options.B: Dhuhr with Asr and Maghrib with Isha may be combined during travel or genuine hardship; Fajr is never combined
options.C: Only Dhuhr and Asr can be combined, never Maghrib and Isha
options.D: Combining prayers is not permitted; it was only allowed during the time of the Prophet (SAW)

explanation: The permissible combining (Jam') of prayers applies to Dhuhr with Asr (praying both at the time of either) and Maghrib with Isha. Fajr is never combined with any other prayer. This is established in authentic hadith and is agreed upon for travel. Scholars differ on whether it is permitted for non-travel hardship such as illness, rain, or extreme circumstances — the majority allow it in genuine necessity.

source.name: Sahih al-Bukhari; Sahih Muslim
source.reference: Sahih al-Bukhari Book 18, Hadith 1056; Sahih Muslim Book 5, Hadith 703

question: What is the concept of Wali (guardian) in Islamic marriage law, and what is its ruling according to the majority of scholars?

options.A: The Wali is an optional witness who may attend the nikah but has no legal authority
options.B: The Wali is a mandatory male guardian whose consent is a condition for the validity of a woman's marriage according to the majority of scholars
options.C: The Wali is the Muslim judge who officiates the marriage contract in a court of law
options.D: The Wali is required only when the bride is a non-Muslim woman marrying a Muslim man

explanation: According to the majority of scholars (Maliki, Shafi'i, Hanbali), the Wali — a woman's male guardian, typically her father or nearest male relative — is a condition for the validity of marriage. The Prophet (SAW) said: 'There is no marriage without a guardian.' The Hanafi madhab holds that an adult, sane woman may contract her own marriage, though the Wali's involvement is strongly preferred. Two witnesses are also required by scholarly consensus.

source.name: Sunan Abu Dawud; Jami at-Tirmidhi
source.reference: Sunan Abu Dawud Book 12, Hadith 2085; Jami at-Tirmidhi Book 11, Hadith 1101
